source_rules: Output is always a self-contained HTML file
Page 1: Core framework and key concepts
Page 2: Practical application, techniques, and competency summary
Page 3: SVG flow diagram connecting all concepts visually

evals.prompt: Create a cheat sheet from this course content: 'Prompt Engineering Fundamentals - covers chain-of-thought reasoning, few-shot prompting, system prompts, temperature settings, and output formatting. Key framework: CRISP (Context, Role, Instructions, Style, Parameters).'
evals.expected_output: A self-contained HTML file with 3 pages covering CRISP framework, techniques, and a flow diagram
assertions: Output contains valid HTML declaration (<!DOCTYPE html> or <html>)
Output contains at least 3 distinct page/section divisions (pages, sections, or divs marked for print page breaks)
Output includes the text 'CRISP' somewhere in the content
Output contains at least one SVG element (inline <svg> tag)
Output contains no external CSS or JS imports from local files (no <link href=..> or <script src=..> to local paths)

evals.prompt: Make a cheat sheet for: 'RAG Architecture Course - Topics: vector databases, embedding models, chunking strategies, retrieval methods, reranking, context window management. Duration: 4 hours.'
evals.expected_output: HTML cheat sheet covering RAG architecture concepts across 3 pages
assertions: Output contains the word 'vector' or 'embedding' at least once
Output contains the word 'chunking' or 'chunk' at least once
Output contains at least 3 heading tags (h1, h2, or h3) with meaningful content
Output contains an SVG element with at least one path, rect, or circle element
Output HTML is over 500 lines (comprehensive 3-page structure, not a stub)

evals.prompt: Generate a reference sheet from: 'AI Safety & Alignment - Module 1: RLHF basics. Module 2: Constitutional AI. Module 3: Red teaming. Module 4: Evaluation frameworks. Key people: Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepMind.'
evals.expected_output: 3-page HTML cheat sheet with AI safety concepts, techniques, and visual diagram
assertions: Output mentions 'RLHF' or 'reinforcement learning' at least once
Output mentions at least 2 of these: 'Constitutional AI', 'Red teaming', 'Evaluation', 'Framework'
Second page or middle section contains practical application language (includes words like 'technique', 'method', 'apply', 'practice', 'implement', or 'use')
Output contains inline CSS styles (style tags or style attributes present, no external stylesheets from local paths)
Output contains at least one SVG visual element or diagram

evals.prompt: Cheat sheet for a very short course: 'Intro to LLMs - 30 minutes. Covers: what are LLMs, tokenization, inference.'
evals.expected_output: Even with minimal input, produces a complete 3-page HTML structure
assertions: Output is valid HTML (contains opening <html> and closing </html> tags)
Output contains at least 3 distinct page sections or divisions despite minimal input
Output mentions 'tokenization', 'token', 'inference', or 'LLM' at least once
Output HTML file is over 200 lines (skill elaborates and structures the minimal input)
Output contains an SVG element or visual diagram on the third page

evals.prompt: Create a cheat sheet from: 'Advanced Claude Code Workflows - Skills architecture, subagent patterns, eval frameworks, memory systems, hook patterns. Taught by Anthropic engineering team. Includes hands-on labs.'
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive 3-page HTML cheat sheet on Claude Code workflows
assertions: Output mentions 'skill', 'skills', 'architecture', or 'pattern' in the content
Output mentions 'subagent', 'agent', 'eval', or 'framework' in the content
Page 1 section contains framework or concept overview with structured definitions
Page 3 section contains an SVG diagram connecting multiple concepts visually
Output does NOT contain placeholder text (no 'TODO', 'INSERT', 'TBD', 'PLACEHOLDER', or '[...]' markers)
